Title: Patient dashboard page load meets P95 response-time SLA under nominal load
Preconditions: Performance test environment provisioned to production-equivalent spec; OpenMRS RefApp deployed with seeded dataset of 50,000 patients; JMeter/Gatling load harness configured; monitoring (APM) active
Test_Steps: 1. Configure load script to open Patient Dashboard for randomized existing patient IDs | 2. Ramp to 50 concurrent virtual users over 2 minutes | 3. Sustain steady state for 10 minutes | 4. Capture server-side and client-side response times | 5. Compute P50, P95, P99 latency
Test_Data: Patients=50000; ConcurrentUsers=50; PatientID pool=synthetic IDs 1001-51000; SLA target P95<=2000ms
Expected_Result: Patient Dashboard P95 response time is <=2000ms and P99 <=3500ms; zero HTTP 5xx errors; results recorded against REQ-PERF-001

Title: REST patient search API meets latency budget at nominal throughput
Preconditions: OpenMRS REST module enabled; 50,000 patients seeded; API auth token provisioned for performance service account; load harness configured against /ws/rest/v1/patient
Test_Steps: 1. Issue GET /ws/rest/v1/patient?q=<term> at 30 requests/sec | 2. Sustain for 10 minutes | 3. Record per-request latency and status codes | 4. Compute percentiles
Expected_Result: API P95 latency <=800ms, P99 <=1500ms, error rate <0.1%; criterion traced to REQ-PERF-002

Title: System sustains target concurrent clinical user load without functional break
Preconditions: Production-equivalent env; realistic mixed workflow script (login, search, open dashboard, enter vitals); 200 named test accounts provisioned
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp from 0 to 200 concurrent users over 10 minutes | 2. Each user executes mixed clinical workflow loop | 3. Sustain 30 minutes | 4. Monitor error rate, latency and throughput | 5. Validate sampled workflows complete functionally
Expected_Result: All workflows complete with functional correctness; aggregate error rate <0.5%; P95 transaction latency within per-transaction SLA; no deadlocks; traced to REQ-PERF-003

Title: Concurrent-user threshold boundary: behavior at exactly rated capacity
Preconditions: Rated concurrent capacity defined as 200 users in NFR; load harness configured for precise user counts
Test_Steps: 1. Run load at 199 users for 10 minutes and record metrics | 2. Run load at exactly 200 users for 10 minutes | 3. Compare latency, error rate, resource utilization at each point
Expected_Result: At 200 users (rated capacity) all SLAs are still met; metrics at 199 and 200 differ within expected linear range; no SLA breach at the boundary; traced to REQ-PERF-003

Title: Overload beyond rated capacity triggers graceful degradation not failure
Preconditions: Rated capacity 200; rate limiting / connection pool limits configured per design; monitoring active
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp load to 300 users (150% of rated capacity) | 2. Sustain 10 minutes | 3. Observe rejection behavior, queueing, and error responses | 4. After test, return to 200 users and verify recovery
Expected_Result: System sheds excess load gracefully (e.g. HTTP 429/503 with retry semantics or queueing) rather than crashing; no data corruption; system recovers to nominal SLA within 5 minutes once load drops; traced to REQ-PERF-009

Title: Patient list pagination latency stays within budget under high data volume
Preconditions: 100,000 patients seeded; paginated patient list/search UI available; load harness configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute search returning large result set | 2. Page through results requesting page sizes per criteria | 3. Measure time-to-first-page and subsequent page latency | 4. Record percentiles across 1000 paging operations
Expected_Result: Each page request returns within P95<=1200ms regardless of page depth; deep pages do not show linear latency growth (keyset/indexed paging); traced to REQ-PERF-004

Title: Deep pagination boundary at maximum page offset
Preconditions: 100,000 result-set query available; max supported page size and offset documented in design
Test_Steps: 1. Request the last page of a 100,000-row result at max page size | 2. Request one page beyond the last valid page | 3. Measure latency and validate response
Expected_Result: Last valid page returns within latency budget; request beyond last page returns empty result set with success status (no error, no timeout); traced to REQ-PERF-004

Title: Search with zero matches returns quickly (negative-result performance)
Preconditions: Large patient dataset seeded; search endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. Execute search for a term guaranteed to match no records | 2. Measure response time | 3. Repeat 100 times under light concurrency
Expected_Result: Empty result returned with P95<=600ms; no full table scan observed in DB plan; traced to REQ-PERF-004

Title: Standard clinical report generates within defined time budget
Preconditions: Reporting module configured; dataset of 50,000 patients with encounters seeded; report definition for patient encounter summary available
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger report generation for a 1-month date range | 2. Measure end-to-end generation time | 3. Repeat for 5 runs and average | 4. Validate output row count correctness
Test_Data: Report=Encounter Summary; DateRange=30 days; Runs=5; SLA<=30s
Expected_Result: Report completes within <=30s for the defined range with correct row counts; no timeout; traced to REQ-PERF-005

Title: Large-range report generation boundary and timeout handling
Preconditions: Reporting configured; 12 months of seeded encounter data; configured report timeout threshold documented
Test_Steps: 1. Generate report for maximum supported range (12 months) | 2. Measure time | 3. Generate report for a range exceeding supported limit | 4. Observe handling
Test_Data: Report=Encounter Summary; MaxRange=12 months; ExcessRange=24 months
Expected_Result: Max-range report completes within configured upper bound; excess-range request is either chunked/queued or rejected with a clear message rather than hanging or OOM; traced to REQ-PERF-005

Title: Concurrent report generation does not starve interactive clinical traffic
Preconditions: Reporting + interactive workflows on same env; resource isolation/queue policy defined
Test_Steps: 1. Establish interactive baseline at 100 users | 2. Concurrently trigger 5 heavy reports | 3. Measure interactive latency during report execution | 4. Vary combinations of report count (0,5,10) vs interactive load (100,200) per decision table | 5. Record SLA pass/fail per cell
Expected_Result: Interactive workflow SLA holds (P95<=2000ms) across decision-table combinations within rated capacity; reports may queue but interactive traffic is prioritized; traced to REQ-PERF-005 and REQ-PERF-009

Title: Patient dashboard load stays within database query-count budget (N+1 guard)
Preconditions: DB query logging / APM SQL capture enabled; OpenMRS dashboard instrumented; single-user trace mode
Test_Steps: 1. Enable SQL statement capture | 2. Load a patient dashboard for one patient | 3. Count distinct SQL statements executed and total DB time | 4. Compare against query budget
Test_Data: QueryBudget<=40 statements per dashboard load; DBTime<=400ms
Expected_Result: Dashboard load executes <=40 SQL statements with no N+1 explosion; total DB time <=400ms; any per-row repeated query is flagged; traced to REQ-PERF-006

Title: Slow-query threshold: no single query exceeds budget under load
Preconditions: DB slow-query log enabled with threshold; load harness configured; representative workload script
Test_Steps: 1. Run mixed workload at rated capacity for 15 minutes | 2. Collect slow-query log entries above threshold | 3. Identify queries lacking index usage | 4. Capture execution plans for top offenders
Expected_Result: No query exceeds 500ms during sustained rated load; all hot-path queries use indexes (no full scans on large tables); traced to REQ-PERF-006

Title: Connection pool exhaustion boundary under peak concurrency
Preconditions: DB connection pool max size configured per design; load harness can drive peak concurrency; pool metrics exposed
Test_Steps: 1. Drive concurrency to saturate the connection pool | 2. Monitor active vs idle vs waiting connections | 3. Push 10% beyond configured pool max | 4. Observe wait-timeout behavior
Expected_Result: At pool saturation, requests queue within configured wait timeout and succeed; beyond capacity, requests fail fast with a clear error rather than hanging indefinitely; no connection leak after test; traced to REQ-PERF-006

Title: 24-hour soak test shows no memory leak or latency drift
Preconditions: Dedicated soak environment; sustained moderate load script; heap/GC and resource monitoring with retention >24h
Test_Steps: 1. Apply 40% of rated load continuously for 24 hours | 2. Sample heap usage, GC frequency, latency hourly | 3. Plot trend lines | 4. Compare hour-1 vs hour-24 metrics
Expected_Result: No upward drift in heap baseline after GC across 24h (no leak); P95 latency at hour-24 within 10% of hour-1; no resource exhaustion; traced to REQ-PERF-007

Title: Endurance stability: error rate remains flat over extended run
Preconditions: Soak environment; long-duration script; error logging aggregated
Test_Steps: 1. Run 8-hour endurance test at 60% rated load | 2. Aggregate error counts per hour | 3. Detect any cumulative error growth or connection leak | 4. Verify thread counts stable
Expected_Result: Hourly error rate stays flat (no cumulative growth); thread and handle counts remain bounded; traced to REQ-PERF-007

Title: CPU utilization stays below threshold at rated load
Preconditions: Production-equivalent sizing; OS/container CPU metrics collected; rated load script ready
Test_Steps: 1. Apply rated load (200 users) for 20 minutes | 2. Sample CPU per app and DB node | 3. Compute sustained average and peak CPU | 4. Compare to thresholds
Expected_Result: Sustained CPU <=70% with headroom; peaks <=85%; no sustained saturation; traced to REQ-PERF-008

Title: Memory and GC pressure stay within thresholds at rated load
Preconditions: JVM heap and GC metrics exposed; rated load script ready; container memory limits set
Test_Steps: 1. Apply rated load for 20 minutes | 2. Sample heap used, GC pause time, GC frequency | 3. Verify no near-limit memory pressure or long GC pauses
Expected_Result: Heap usage stays <=75% of limit; no GC pause >200ms; no OutOfMemory; traced to REQ-PERF-008

Title: Resource threshold pairwise coverage across node/load/cache combinations
Preconditions: Multiple env configurations available (app nodes, load levels, cache on/off); test matrix tool used to derive pairwise set
Test_Steps: 1. Generate pairwise combinations of AppNodes{1,2}, Load{nominal,peak}, Cache{on,off} | 2. Execute each selected combination for 10 minutes | 3. Record CPU, memory, latency per run | 4. Validate thresholds per run
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination meets resource and latency thresholds; cache-off + peak is the worst case and still bounded; traced to REQ-PERF-008

Title: Dependency slowdown (DB latency injection) degrades gracefully
Preconditions: Fault-injection capability to add DB latency; circuit-breaker/timeout config per design; monitoring active
Test_Steps: 1. Baseline at nominal load (state: Normal) | 2. Inject 500ms DB latency (state: Degraded) | 3. Observe timeouts, retries, user-facing behavior | 4. Remove latency injection (state: Recovering) | 5. Confirm return to Normal
Expected_Result: System transitions Normal->Degraded with bounded user impact (timeouts surface clean errors, no cascading crash), then Recovering->Normal once fault clears within 2 minutes; traced to REQ-PERF-009

Title: Downstream service unavailable falls back without functional break
Preconditions: A non-critical downstream integration (e.g. lab results feed) can be disabled; fallback design defined
Test_Steps: 1. Establish nominal load | 2. Make downstream service unreachable | 3. Exercise workflows depending on it | 4. Verify fallback or clear unavailability messaging | 5. Restore service
Expected_Result: Core clinical workflows continue; dependent feature shows graceful unavailable state, not a crash or hang; no PHI loss; recovery automatic on restore; traced to REQ-PERF-009

Title: Vitals entry remains functionally correct under sustained load
Preconditions: Rated load running; vitals entry workflow scripted; data verification queries prepared
Test_Steps: 1. Under 80% rated load, submit 500 vitals entries with known values | 2. After test, query persisted observations | 3. Compare submitted vs stored values | 4. Check for partial writes or duplicates
Expected_Result: All 500 vitals persisted exactly with no data loss, duplication, or value corruption under load; traced to REQ-PERF-010

Title: Concurrent order entry maintains data integrity (no lost updates)
Preconditions: Order entry workflow available; concurrency harness can target same and different patients; DB integrity checks ready
Test_Steps: 1. Drive concurrent order submissions for overlapping patients | 2. Include simultaneous edits to same encounter | 3. After run, verify no lost updates, no orphan records, referential integrity intact
Expected_Result: No lost updates or race-induced corruption; optimistic/locking behavior preserves integrity; all FK constraints satisfied; traced to REQ-PERF-010

Title: FHIR Patient read endpoint meets latency SLA under load
Preconditions: FHIR2 module enabled (or HAPI FHIR adapter); 50,000 Patient resources available; load harness targeting /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient
Test_Steps: 1. Issue GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/<id> at 25 RPS | 2. Sustain 10 minutes | 3. Validate FHIR response conformance and latency | 4. Compute percentiles
Expected_Result: FHIR read P95<=900ms with valid R4 Patient resources; error rate <0.1%; traced to REQ-PERF-011

Title: FHIR search bundle pagination performance under volume
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with large Observation dataset; _count and pagination links supported
Test_Steps: 1. Execute GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation?patient=<id>&_count=50 | 2. Follow next-link pages | 3. Measure per-page latency and bundle correctness across deep pages
Expected_Result: Each FHIR search page returns within P95<=1500ms with correct next/self links and total handling; no degradation on deep pages; traced to REQ-PERF-011

Title: FHIR Bundle transaction throughput and atomicity under load
Preconditions: FHIR transaction endpoint available; bundle payloads prepared; concurrency harness ready
Test_Steps: 1. POST transaction Bundles at 10 RPS for 10 minutes | 2. Each bundle creates Patient+Encounter+Observation | 3. Verify atomic commit and latency | 4. Check for partial commits on any error
Expected_Result: Transaction bundles commit atomically within P95<=2000ms; no partial commits; throughput sustained; traced to REQ-PERF-011

Title: HL7 v2 ADT message ingestion throughput meets criteria
Preconditions: HL7 listener/interface engine configured (or omiiCARE adapter); MLLP feeder available; message backlog generator ready
Test_Steps: 1. Feed ADT^A01 messages at 50 msgs/sec for 10 minutes | 2. Measure end-to-end processing latency and ACK turnaround | 3. Verify no message loss and queue depth stays bounded
Expected_Result: All messages processed with ACK within P95<=1000ms; queue depth bounded; zero message loss; traced to REQ-PERF-012

Title: HL7 burst-load boundary and backpressure handling
Preconditions: HL7 interface configured with queue depth limits; burst generator available
Test_Steps: 1. Send a sudden burst at 3x nominal rate for 2 minutes | 2. Observe queue depth, NACK/backpressure behavior | 3. Return to nominal | 4. Verify backlog drains without loss
Expected_Result: Burst is absorbed via queueing/backpressure with no message loss; backlog drains to zero after burst; no NACK storm or crash; traced to REQ-PERF-012

Title: Cache hit improves response time and reduces DB load (positive case)
Preconditions: Caching layer enabled for reference/concept data; cache metrics exposed; load harness ready
Test_Steps: 1. Warm cache via initial requests | 2. Run repeated reads of cacheable data under load | 3. Measure cache hit ratio and latency vs cold baseline | 4. Confirm DB query reduction
Expected_Result: Cache hit ratio >=90% in steady state; cached-path latency markedly lower than cold; DB query volume reduced; traced to REQ-PERF-013

Title: Cold cache / cache stampede behavior under concurrent miss
Preconditions: Caching enabled; ability to flush cache; concurrency harness ready
Test_Steps: 1. Flush cache | 2. Simultaneously fire many requests for the same uncached key | 3. Observe whether a stampede of DB queries occurs | 4. Measure latency spike and recovery
Expected_Result: Cache miss is coalesced (single-flight) or limited; no thundering-herd DB overload; latency spike bounded and recovers; traced to REQ-PERF-013

Title: Authentication and session creation latency under login storm
Preconditions: Auth subsystem configured; many test accounts available; load harness can simulate simultaneous logins
Test_Steps: 1. Simulate a morning login storm of 200 logins within 60 seconds | 2. Measure auth latency and success rate | 3. Verify session tokens issued correctly | 4. Confirm no auth bypass or lockout misfire
Expected_Result: All logins authenticate within P95<=1500ms; 100% correct success/failure decisions; no spurious lockouts; no auth bypass under load; traced to REQ-PERF-014

Title: Rate limiting / throttling prevents abuse without harming legit traffic
Preconditions: Rate limiting configured per design; harness can generate abusive and legitimate traffic concurrently
Test_Steps: 1. Generate abusive request flood from one client identity | 2. Concurrently run legitimate user traffic | 3. Verify abuser is throttled (429) while legit traffic stays within SLA
Expected_Result: Abusive client receives throttling responses; legitimate traffic SLA unaffected; no global outage from one bad actor; traced to REQ-PERF-014

Title: Audit log write does not breach latency budget on PHI access under load
Preconditions: HIPAA audit logging enabled for PHI access; load harness exercising patient record views; audit store monitored
Test_Steps: 1. Drive PHI-access workflows at rated load for 15 minutes | 2. Verify every access produces an audit record | 3. Measure added latency from audit write | 4. Confirm no audit records dropped
Expected_Result: Audit logging adds <=50ms overhead; zero audit records lost under load; access latency still within SLA; traced to REQ-PERF-015

Title: Large clinical payload (bulk observation) handling within budget
Preconditions: Endpoint accepting bulk observation submission; payload size limits documented
Test_Steps: 1. Submit increasing payload sizes up to documented max | 2. Submit one payload just over the max | 3. Measure latency at max and observe rejection behavior over max
Test_Data: MaxPayload=5MB; Over=6MB; Observations per payload up to 1000
Expected_Result: At max payload, request succeeds within budget; over-max is rejected cleanly with a clear 413-style error rather than timeout/crash; traced to REQ-PERF-002

Title: Horizontal scale-out maintains SLA (decision table of nodes vs load)
Preconditions: Cluster can run with 1, 2, 3 app nodes behind load balancer; shared DB; load harness configurable
Test_Steps: 1. For each node count, run load levels {nominal, peak, overload} | 2. Record SLA pass/fail per cell of node x load matrix | 3. Confirm scale-out restores SLA where single node fails
Expected_Result: SLA pass/fail matrix shows adding nodes recovers SLA at peak/overload; near-linear throughput scaling within tested range; traced to REQ-PERF-016

Title: Application cold-start and readiness time within boot SLA
Preconditions: Ability to restart app node; readiness probe defined; timing instrumentation
Test_Steps: 1. Stop app node | 2. Start node and timestamp until readiness probe passes | 3. Issue first real request and measure first-request latency | 4. Repeat 3 times
Expected_Result: Node reaches ready state within <=120s; first post-boot request completes within <=3000ms (warm-up allowance); traced to REQ-PERF-017

Title: Node failover under load preserves in-flight criteria and recovers SLA
Preconditions: Multi-node cluster with load balancer; session/failover design defined; load running
Test_Steps: 1. Run nominal load across cluster (state: Healthy) | 2. Kill one app node mid-load (state: Degraded) | 3. Observe LB redistribution and in-flight request handling | 4. Confirm SLA recovery (state: Recovered)
Expected_Result: On node loss, traffic redistributes; in-flight requests fail over or surface clean retryable errors (no PHI corruption); SLA recovers within 60s; traced to REQ-PERF-016

Title: Exploratory performance hot-spot hunt across clinical workflows
Preconditions: APM/profiler attached; seeded realistic data; tester familiar with clinical workflows; time-boxed charter
Test_Steps: 1. Time-box 90 minutes | 2. Exercise diverse workflows (registration, encounter, orders, reports, search) while watching APM traces | 3. Note any operation exceeding perceived-instant threshold | 4. Capture worst traces and resource spikes | 5. Log candidate bottlenecks
Expected_Result: Documented list of slowest operations with traces and reproducible steps; at least the top hot-spots identified for follow-up; no critical workflow silently exceeds budget; traced to REQ-PERF-001

Title: Assistive-tech-driven workflow remains responsive under nominal load
Preconditions: Screen-reader/keyboard-only test path defined; background nominal load applied; accessibility tooling ready
Test_Steps: 1. Apply nominal background load | 2. Complete a keyboard-only patient search and dashboard open with screen reader | 3. Measure perceived responsiveness and focus-update latency | 4. Confirm no UI freeze blocking AT
Expected_Result: Accessible workflow stays responsive under load with focus and live-region updates within perceptible budget; no long-task freeze; traced to REQ-PERF-001

Title: Throughput ceiling discovery (stress to breaking point) for capacity model
Preconditions: Stress environment isolated from shared systems; monitoring with high retention; staged ramp script
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp load in stages beyond rated capacity until SLA breaches | 2. Record the user count and throughput at first SLA breach (knee) and at saturation | 3. Identify the limiting resource (CPU/DB/pool) | 4. Document capacity headroom vs rated
Test_Data: StartUsers=200; Ramp step=+50; Until SLA breach
Expected_Result: Knee point and max throughput identified with the bottleneck resource named; rated capacity confirmed to have documented headroom margin; traced to REQ-PERF-016

Title: Pairwise mixed-workload combinations meet aggregate SLA
Preconditions: Multiple workflow types scriptable; pairwise tool to derive mix combinations; load harness
Test_Steps: 1. Derive pairwise set across WorkflowMix{search-heavy,write-heavy,report-heavy}, Cache{on,off}, DataVolume{50k,100k} | 2. Run each selected combo at nominal load | 3. Validate aggregate SLA per combo
Expected_Result: Each pairwise workload combination meets aggregate SLA; worst-case combo identified and still within criteria or flagged; traced to REQ-PERF-003

Title: Response time degradation across growing data volumes stays sub-linear
Preconditions: Datasets seeded at 10k, 50k, 100k, 500k patients on comparable envs; same query workload
Test_Steps: 1. Run identical search/dashboard workload against each dataset size | 2. Record P95 latency per size | 3. Plot latency vs volume | 4. Verify growth is sub-linear (indexed access)
Expected_Result: Latency growth is sub-linear across volumes (no full-scan blowup); 500k dataset still within or near SLA with documented margin; traced to REQ-PERF-004

Title: Malformed/unauthorized API requests are rejected fast under load
Preconditions: REST/FHIR endpoints available; harness can send malformed and unauthorized requests; monitoring active
Test_Steps: 1. Under nominal load, inject 10% malformed and 10% unauthorized requests | 2. Measure rejection latency and ensure they do not consume disproportionate resources | 3. Confirm valid-request SLA unaffected
Expected_Result: Invalid requests are rejected quickly (4xx) without heavy processing; they do not degrade valid-request SLA; no resource amplification from bad input; traced to REQ-PERF-002
